display.purpose: Currently playing track. Most prominent element on screen. textWrap: balance.

headline.purpose: Artist. Subordinate to track name through weight (500 vs 600), not size. textWrap: balance.

title.purpose: Album. Only serif-italic use in the system. Set in accent color.

catalog.purpose: Catalog footer line only: year · label · catalog number. 13px — one step above Label for room-monitor legibility at 6–10ft.

label.purpose: Status strip, PREV/NEXT labels, side/position counter, status indicator. 11px uppercase. All instrumental metadata except the catalog footer.

shadows.name: cover-lift
shadows.purpose: Album cover container only. Lifts the record from the background.

shadows.name: cover-ring
shadows.purpose: Hairline inset on the cover. Keeps edge visible on near-black backgrounds.

shadows.name: status-glow
shadows.purpose: Status dot glow during playing and boot states. Parameterized by accent color.

motion.name: pulse
motion.purpose: Status dot during playing/boot state. Must have prefers-reduced-motion: none override.

motion.name: rotate
motion.purpose: Boot-state spinner arc. Must have prefers-reduced-motion: none override.

components.name: Genre Chip
components.description: Non-interactive genre classification. Monospaced, low-contrast border, transparent background.
components.css: .ds-genre-chip { display: inline-block; padding: 5px 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: #8a857c; border: 1px solid rgba(138,133,124,0.33); border-radius: 2px; white-space: nowrap; background: transparent; }

components.name: Status Indicator
components.description: Pulsing dot + uppercase mono label. Color and animation vary by playback state.
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #8a857c; } .ds-status-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: #8a857c; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-status--playing .ds-status-dot { background: #c8c8c8; box-shadow: 0 0 8px #c8c8c8; animation: ds-pulse 1.6s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } 50% { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.9); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-status-dot { animation: none !important; } }

components.name: Accent Rule
components.description: 64×2px horizontal punctuation mark between track name and artist name. Fixed width, not full-span.

components.name: Now Playing Display
components.description: Full 1024×600 artboard. Cover left (440×440), metadata right. Hard-fixed dimensions for companion screen deployment.
components.html:
Catholic Block
Sonic Youth
Sister
Noise RockAlt Rock
1987 · SST Records · SST-134
components.css: .ds-display { width: 1024px; height: 600px; position: relative; background: #0a0a0a; color: #ebe6dc; overflow: hidden; font-family: 'Inter Tight', sans-serif; display: grid; grid-template-columns: 440px 1fr; gap: 44px; padding: 60px 50px 40px; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-display-cover { width: 440px; height: 440px; box-shadow: 0 30px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.55), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.04); } .ds-display-meta { display: flex; flex-direction: column; padding-top: 6px; min-width: 0; } .ds-display-track { font-size: 72px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 0.98; letter-spacing: -0.03em; color: #ebe6dc; text-wrap: balance; margin-bottom: 22px; overflow: hidden; display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 3; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; } .ds-display-artist { font-size: 48px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.04; letter-spacing: -0.022em; color: #ebe6dc; text-wrap: balance; margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-display-album { font-size: 32px; font-family: 'Newsreader', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; color: #c8c8c8; line-height: 1.12; margin-bottom: 24px; overflow: hidden; display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; } .ds-display-chips { display: flex; gap: 6px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: auto; min-height: 28px; } .ds-display-catalog { margin-top: auto; padding-top: 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #8a857c; }

components.name: Idle Screen
components.description: Full 1024×600 artboard shown when no record is on the platter. Currently a placeholder (repeating diagonal stripe + 'NO RECORD ON PLATTER' label in JetBrains Mono). v1.4.0 redesign planned: grid of recent album covers, optional clock/date, random Discogs collection suggestion. Must use fallback palette (#0a0a0a bg) and preserve sharp-corner vocabulary.
components.html: NO RECORD ON PLATTER
components.css: .ds-idle { width: 1024px; height: 600px; background: repeating-linear-gradient(135deg, #161616 0px, #161616 20px, #0a0a0a 20px, #0a0a0a 40px); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-idle-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #8a857c; }

narrative.overview:
A screen that earns its place next to a turntable and good speakers. It sits quietly in the room while the record plays — noticeable when glanced at, invisible when not. The design doesn't perform. It informs.

Each record defines its own visual world. The palette is extracted from the cover and applied to every surface — background, text, accent, depth — with a smooth 1-second transition between albums. Metadata surfaces from your Discogs collection first, yielding specific pressing details (catalog number, label, tracklist, side positions) that make the display feel like it knows your record rather than any record. Typography carries the same logic. The track name is large and tight because it is the thing you want to know. The artist confirms it. The album title is set in a warm italic serif because it is the one element that requires warmth.

This system explicitly rejects the streaming-app aesthetic: no rounded tiles, no soft gradient scrubbers, no playback chrome.
keyCharacteristics: Per-album palette theming: every record tints its own display
Sharp corners throughout; no decorative rounding
Monospaced labels for all technical/metadata text
Deep ambient shadow under cover art; tonal layering for depth
Compressed display typography; generous element breathing room
Static fallback palette for boot/idle states; smooth 1-second lerp between albums on track change

rules.name: The Full-Opacity Rule
rules.body: Secondary text (muted) already conveys its subdued role through hue. Never compound with opacity. p.muted at opacity 0.65 over a dark bg fails contrast. Use the color as-is.

rules.name: The Per-Album Rule
rules.body: The five palette roles (bg, surface, accent, text, muted) are architecture, not values. Treat the fallback palette as the null state; each album's extraction is the real design.

rules.name: The Hue Diversity Rule
rules.body: Extracted accent colors must maintain ≥60° hue separation (OKLCH/LCH space) from every other album's accent. Unconstrained extraction clusters toward warm orange (hue 40–60°). When a new extraction lands within 60° of an existing accent, use the cover's second-most-prominent hue or shift ±60°+ preserving lightness and chroma.

rules.name: The Mono Firewall Rule
rules.body: JetBrains Mono is for data. Track names and artist names get Inter Tight. Album titles get Newsreader. Never set a track name in mono; never set catalog data in a serif.

rules.name: The One Serif Rule
rules.body: Newsreader italic appears exactly once per artboard: the album title. Adding it anywhere else dissolves the distinction.

rules.name: The Flat-Except-Cover Rule
rules.body: Shadows belong to the album cover, not to cards, panels, or text elements. The cover is the physical object; it gets lift. Everything else is flat.

dos: Do use the muted role at full opacity for secondary text.
Do enforce ≥60° hue separation between any two albums' accent colors in data.js. Unconstrained extraction clusters toward warm orange; the collection needs to span the hue wheel.
Do set alt on album cover images to '{artist} — {album}'.
Do include prefers-reduced-motion guards on every continuous animation.
Do protect long album titles from overflow with -webkit-line-clamp: 2.
Do protect long track names from overflow with -webkit-line-clamp: 3. Production track names can be long.
Do cap genre chip display at 3 with a +N overflow indicator, and set minHeight: 28 on the chip container. Discogs can return 0 or 5+ genres.
Do keep the five palette roles semantic (bg, surface, accent, text, muted).
Do verify muted/bg contrast ratio (≥4.5:1) for every new album palette. Cool-dark backgrounds reduce contrast faster than neutral darks.
donts: Don't introduce the streaming-app aesthetic: no rounded tiles, no progress bars, no playback scrubbers, no media controls.
Don't show elapsed time or track duration. The display is static by design.
Don't use generic music visualization decoration (waveform bars, equalizer visualizers, pulsing blobs).
Don't add rounded corners to the artboard, cover container, or metadata panel.
Don't set album title or track name in JetBrains Mono.
Don't add a second serif-italic element. Newsreader italic earns its warmth precisely because it appears once.
Don't apply border-left or border-right as a colored stripe on any element.
